title: Complex Line Bundle Of Filled States
summary: For a two-band system with one filled band, the occupied state at each regular momentum defines a one-dimensional complex vector space; together these fibers form the line bundle on which the Berry connection lives.
assumptions: Exactly one band is filled in the local two-band discussion.
Bad points are removed from the base space.
scope: Lecture One geometric reformulation of the occupied-state phase problem.

source_anchors.notes: The line-bundle picture underlies the Berry connection construction.

symmetries: U(1) phase redundancy in local eigenvector choices.
representation: Rank-one complex vector bundle over regular momentum space.
motivation: This note exists because Berry transport is not defined on a bare set of eigenvectors. The geometric object underneath the phase of the filled state is a rank-one complex bundle, and every later Berry formula is really a statement about that bundle.
prerequisite_story: Start from a gapped two-band Hamiltonian with one filled state at each regular momentum. A normalized eigenvector can be chosen locally, but not necessarily globally with one smooth phase convention, so the occupied states must be organized as fibers rather than as one globally fixed vector.
local_context_story: In Witten Lecture One, the line-bundle viewpoint is the bridge from local eigenvectors to geometry. Once the occupied state over each regular momentum is treated as a complex line, the Berry connection becomes an honest U(1) connection on that bundle.
forward_links_story: This bundle is the immediate prerequisite for the Berry connection, whose curvature then produces the local node charge and eventually the first-Chern-class interpretation of the TKNN invariant.
canonical_questions: Why does one filled band define a line bundle instead of a single globally fixed wavefunction?
What exactly fails at a bad point, and why must the base space be punctured there?
Why is the Berry connection abelian only in the rank-one case?
scope_boundaries: This note is rank-one: several filled bands produce a higher-rank bundle and a non-abelian Berry connection.
The note concerns regular momentum points only; it does not claim a global smooth section exists across a band-touching singularity.

math_expressions.notes: For one filled band, the occupied fiber at regular momentum p is a one-dimensional complex vector space.

symbols.meaning: a local normalized filled-band eigenvector spanning the occupied fiber

failure_modes: For multiple filled bands the geometry becomes non-abelian rather than a line bundle.
retrieval_hints: Use for geometric explanations of Berry phase and curvature.
